varieties.name: Eucalyptus tereticornis (Forest Red Gum, Queensland Blue Gum)
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia, indigenous to humid subtropical climates region, 1800s cultivation
varieties.traits: Tall tree 30-50m height. Smooth white-grey bark shedding seasonally. Lance-shaped glossy green leaves. Open canopy habit.
varieties.availability: Widely available Queensland nurseries, humid subtropical climates City Council revegetation programs, Paten Park Native Nursery

varieties.name: Eucalyptus grandis (Flooded Gum, Rose Gum)
varieties.origin: Queensland and NSW coastal forests, indigenous to subtropical regions
varieties.traits: Very tall forest tree 43-70m. Smooth bark grey-blue-green with flowing patterns. Straight clean trunk to 2/3 height. Thin deciduous bark shedding in strips.
varieties.availability: Major Queensland forestry plantations, humid subtropical climates region nurseries, Delivertree AU

varieties.name: Corymbia maculata (Spotted Gum)
varieties.origin: SE Queensland to southern NSW, formerly Eucalyptus maculata, transferred to Corymbia 1995
varieties.traits: Medium-tall tree 30-60m. Distinctive smooth mottled bark (pinkish-grey, bluish-grey) creating spotted appearance. Dimpled bark texture. Lance-shaped glossy leaves.
varieties.availability: Specialty Trees AU, humid subtropical climates Plant Nursery, The Seed Vine, Australian Plants Online

varieties.name: Corymbia citriodora subsp. variegata (Lemon-Scented Spotted Gum)
varieties.origin: Subtropical Queensland, Cairns to northern NSW
varieties.traits: 30-45m tall. Smooth mottled bark (non-lemon scented variety). Narrow lance-shaped leaves. Peltate juvenile leaves.
varieties.availability: Queensland Forestry plantations, regional nurseries specializing in spotted gums

varieties.name: Eucalyptus saligna (Sydney Blue Gum)
varieties.origin: Eastern NSW and southern Queensland coastal forests, 1800s forestry
varieties.traits: Very tall 60m+ tree. Smooth grey bark with bluish-grey markings above short rough stocking. Excellent form.
varieties.availability: Queensland forestry suppliers, humid subtropical climates region specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus robusta (Swamp Mahogany)
varieties.origin: SE Queensland to NSW coast, offshore islands (Fraser, Moreton, Stradbroke), 1790s European documentation
varieties.traits: 20-30m tall. Thick spongy reddish-brown stringy bark. Broad dark green glossy leaves forming dense canopy.
varieties.availability: Queensland coastal nurseries, humid subtropical climates City Council wetland restoration programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus microcorys (Tallowwood)
varieties.origin: Fraser Island (K'gari) Queensland south to NSW, described 1860
varieties.traits: 40-70m tall. Rough fibrous brownish stringy bark. Glossy green lance-shaped leaves.
varieties.availability: Queensland forestry suppliers, specialized timber species nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus pilularis (Blackbutt)
varieties.origin: Coastal NSW to southern Queensland, historic Queensland timber species
varieties.traits: Tall tree 30-50m. Rough bark at base, smooth pale grey-white above. Distinctive black butt base.
varieties.availability: Redland Shire conservation programs, Queensland coastal forest nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus melliodora (Yellow Box, Honey Box)
varieties.origin: Western Victoria to southern Queensland tablelands, 1825 documentation
varieties.traits: Medium tree to 30m. Variable bark: fibrous yellow-grey on trunk or smooth yellowish above. Blue-green to green leaves.
varieties.availability: Queensland Native Seeds, revegetation projects NSW-Queensland border region

varieties.name: Eucalyptus camaldulensis (River Red Gum)
varieties.origin: Most widely distributed eucalypt across all mainland Australian states, 1832 formal description
varieties.traits: 20-45m tall. Smooth white-cream bark with yellow, pink, brown patches. Lance-shaped leaves. Multiple subspecies.
varieties.availability: Widely available Queensland nurseries, humid subtropical climates waterway restoration programs

varieties.name: Corymbia henryi (Large-Leafed Spotted Gum)
varieties.origin: Whiporie NSW to Helidon Queensland
varieties.traits: Similar to C. maculata but with coarser leaves (2.2-4.5cm wide), larger buds and fruit (0.9-1.6cm). Mottled bark.
varieties.availability: Queensland forestry trials, regional spotted gum nurseries

varieties.name: Corymbia tessellaris (Moreton Bay Ash, Carbeen)
varieties.origin: Coastal Queensland including Moreton Bay region, traditional Aboriginal use
varieties.traits: Medium tree 15-30m. Tessellated (checkered) bark pattern distinctive. White flowers in large terminal panicles.
varieties.availability: humid subtropical climates Native Plant Nurseries, Queensland Indigenous plant suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus curtisii (Plunkett Mallee)
varieties.origin: Restricted endemic 60km south of humid subtropical climates near Plunkett
varieties.traits: Mallee shrub or small tree 2-7m. Multiple stems from lignotuber. Spiky cream-white flowers December-January.
varieties.availability: Specialized humid subtropical climates region native nurseries, conservation programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus kabiana (Mt Beerwah Mallee)
varieties.origin: Glass House Mountains, Sunshine Coast region north of humid subtropical climates
varieties.traits: Dwarf mallee reaching only 6m in 7 years. Koala food species. Compact growth ideal for urban areas.
varieties.availability: Moreton Bay Regional Council, Pine Rivers Koala Care, University of Sunshine Coast programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus crebra (Narrow-Leaved Ironbark)
varieties.origin: Widespread Queensland to northern NSW
varieties.traits: Medium tree 15-25m. Hard dark grey deeply furrowed ironbark. Narrow grey-green leaves. White flowers.
varieties.availability: Queensland forestry suppliers, western humid subtropical climates region nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus propinqua (Small-Fruited Grey Gum)
varieties.origin: Coastal Queensland and northern NSW
varieties.traits: Tall tree 25-45m. Smooth grey bark shedding in long ribbons. White to cream flowers.
varieties.availability: Queensland coastal forestry, subtropical region nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus siderophloia (Coastal Grey Ironbark)
varieties.origin: Coastal NSW to subtropical Queensland, historical timber species
varieties.traits: Large tree to 40m+. Dark grey deeply furrowed hard ironbark to smallest branches. Diamond-shaped buds. Grey-green leaves.
varieties.availability: Queensland coastal nurseries, humid subtropical climates region timber species suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus acmenoides (White Mahogany)
varieties.origin: Coastal Queensland to northern NSW
varieties.traits: Large tree 30-45m. Rough fibrous grey-brown bark. Lance-shaped green leaves. White flowers in clusters.
varieties.availability: Queensland tropical-subtropical forestry suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus resinifera (Red Mahogany)
varieties.origin: Coastal southern Queensland to northern NSW
varieties.traits: Medium-large tree 30-45m. Thick red-brown spongy fibrous bark. Dark green glossy leaves.
varieties.availability: Queensland coastal forestry programs, humid subtropical climates region specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus bancroftii (Bancroft's Red Gum)
varieties.origin: Coastal southern Queensland
varieties.traits: Medium tree. Smooth bark. Known to hybridize with E. robusta and E. tereticornis.
varieties.availability: Specialized conservation nurseries, Queensland indigenous plant programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus paniculata (Grey Ironbark)
varieties.origin: Coastal Queensland to NSW, historical timber since 1800s
varieties.traits: Medium-tall tree 25-40m. Hard grey deeply furrowed ironbark. Dark green leaves. Large white flower panicles.
varieties.availability: Queensland forestry, humid subtropical climates region timber species nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus fibrosa (Red Ironbark)
varieties.origin: Coastal Queensland south through NSW
varieties.traits: Medium tree 20-30m. Deeply furrowed reddish-brown to grey ironbark. Dull green leaves.
varieties.availability: Queensland regional nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus drepanophylla (Grey Ironbark)
varieties.origin: Queensland inland to coastal areas
varieties.traits: Small-medium tree 8-20m. Dark grey ironbark. Sickle-shaped (drepan-) leaves. White flowers.
varieties.availability: Queensland native plant nurseries

varieties.name: Lophostemon confertus (Brush Box)
varieties.origin: Coastal Queensland to NSW, formerly Tristania conferta, Myrtaceae family (related to Eucalyptus)
varieties.traits: Large tree 15-30m. Rough brown tessellated bark. Dark glossy green leaves. White flowers in clusters.
varieties.availability: Widely available humid subtropical climates nurseries, humid subtropical climates City Council street tree programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus tereticornis (Forest Red Gum, Mysore Gum)
varieties.origin: Australian native, introduced to India 1843, extensively planted Karnataka and tropical monsoon climates
varieties.traits: Tall tree 30-50m. Smooth bark white-grey. Lance-shaped leaves. Fast-growing.
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout tropical monsoon climates - State Forest Departments, tropical monsoon climates Forest Research Institute Peechi, private nurseries, farm forestry programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus Hybrid (Mysore Gum)
varieties.origin: Developed in India, form of E. tereticornis, most outstanding variety in Indian conditions since 1950s
varieties.traits: Fast-growing 30-45m. Capable of overtopping weeds. Excellent coppicing. Fire hardy.
varieties.yield_notes: Superior growth in Indian conditions. 5-10m³/ha/year typical tropical monsoon climates plantations. 30-50MT in 5-year clonal plantations.
varieties.availability: Extensively available - tropical monsoon climates Forest Corporation, tropical monsoon climates Forest Research Institute, State Forest Departments, farm forestry suppliers throughout tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Eucalyptus grandis (Flooded Gum, Rose Gum)
varieties.origin: Australian Queensland/NSW, extensively planted tropical monsoon climates high rainfall ranges since 1958
varieties.traits: Very tall 50-70m potential. Smooth bark shedding in strips. Straight trunk excellent form.
varieties.yield_notes: Fast-growing in tropics. Brazil achieves 20-35m³/ha/year on 5-10 year rotations. Intensive breeding raises to 33-70m³/ha/year.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Forest Research Institute Peechi, high-elevation forestry programs, Western Ghats nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus globulus (Blue Gum, Tasmanian Blue Gum)
varieties.origin: Tasmania Australia, introduced India 1800s, historic plantations tropical monsoon climates hills
varieties.traits: Large tree to 30m in India. Smooth peeling bark. Juvenile leaves bluish-green opposite. Adult leaves green alternate lanceolate 6-12 inches.
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates highland nurseries, Nilgiri plantations neighboring Tamil Nadu, essential oil plantation programs, tropical monsoon climates Forest Research Institute

varieties.name: Eucalyptus citriodora (Lemon-Scented Gum)
varieties.origin: Queensland Australia, introduced India for essential oil production
varieties.traits: Tall tree 25-40m. Smooth powdery white bark. Lemon-scented foliage. Narrow lanceolate leaves.

varieties.name: Eucalyptus camaldulensis (River Red Gum)
varieties.origin: Most widespread Australian eucalypt, extensively tested India since 1800s
varieties.traits: Medium-large tree 20-45m. Smooth white-cream bark. Multiple subspecies. Long-lived.
varieties.yield_notes: 5-10m³/ha/year in drier tropics, up to 30m³/ha/year moister regions. Tolerates drought.
varieties.availability: Indian State Forest Departments, farm forestry programs, drought-tolerant species suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus robusta (Swamp Mahogany)
varieties.origin: SE Queensland-NSW Australia, widely planted tropical/subtropical climates globally
varieties.traits: 20-30m tree. Thick spongy reddish-brown bark. Dense canopy broad dark green leaves. Large beaked flower buds.
varieties.availability: Tropical plantation programs, tropical monsoon climates lowland forestry, wetland restoration suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus saligna (Sydney Blue Gum)
varieties.origin: Eastern Australian coastal forests, planted worldwide tropics/subtropics
varieties.traits: Tall tree 50-60m+. Smooth grey-bluish bark. Excellent form. High-quality timber.
varieties.availability: High-rainfall forestry programs, tropical monsoon climates Western Ghats nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus urophylla (Timor Mountain Gum)
varieties.origin: Timor, Indonesia mountains, major tropical plantation species
varieties.traits: Large tree to 50m height, 2m diameter. Mountain species. Straight trunk.
varieties.yield_notes: 20-30m³/ha/year. Brazil achieves 33-70m³/ha/year with intensive breeding. Fast-growing.
varieties.availability: Highland forestry programs, Western Ghats elevation plantations, tropical plantation suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus deglupta (Rainbow Eucalyptus, Mindanao Gum)
varieties.origin: Philippines, Indonesia, Papua New Guinea - ONLY Eucalyptus native to Northern Hemisphere rainforest
varieties.traits: Very tall 35-78m. Famous multi-colored bark (green, blue, purple, orange, maroon) peeling in strips. Smooth orange-tinted bark.
varieties.yield_notes: One of world's fastest-growing trees. Excellent timber quality.

varieties.name: Eucalyptus pellita (Red Mahogany)
varieties.origin: Papua New Guinea, northern Queensland, tropical humid lowlands
varieties.traits: Medium-large tree 30-45m. Rough fibrous bark. Fast-growing in humid tropics.
varieties.availability: Tropical lowland forestry programs, humid climate species suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus crebra (Narrow-Leaf Ironbark)
varieties.origin: Queensland Australia, tested extensively India
varieties.traits: Medium tree 15-25m. Dark grey deeply furrowed ironbark. Narrow grey-green leaves.
varieties.availability: Indian forestry trial programs, drought-tolerant species suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus botryoides x tereticornis
varieties.origin: Hybrid identified in India, tested Karnataka (neighboring tropical monsoon climates)
varieties.traits: Combines traits of both parent species. Variable characteristics.

varieties.name: Eucalyptus robusta x tereticornis
varieties.origin: Natural and cultivated hybrid identified India
varieties.traits: Intermediate characteristics. May combine swamp tolerance with fast growth.
varieties.availability: Research institutions, hybrid development programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus major (Various hybrids)
varieties.origin: Australian species, tested in India including Karnataka
varieties.availability: Forestry research stations, trial program suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus intermedia
varieties.origin: Australian species, evaluated in Indian forestry programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus polyanthemos (Red Box)
varieties.origin: SE Australia, tested in India
varieties.traits: Small-medium tree. Juvenile leaves orbicular, adult lanceolate. Silver-grey foliage.
varieties.availability: Ornamental plant suppliers, drought-tolerant species programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus tessellaris
varieties.origin: Northern Australia tropical regions, tested India
varieties.traits: Medium tree. Tessellated (checkered) bark pattern. Tropical adaptation.
varieties.availability: Tropical species forestry programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus species for Essential Oil (Nilgiri Oil)
varieties.availability: Essential oil plantation programs, Ayurvedic medicine suppliers, tropical monsoon climates agricultural cooperatives

varieties.name: Eucalyptus for Honey Production (Multiple Species)
varieties.availability: Industrial forestry programs, tropical monsoon climates Forest Corporation, farm forestry cooperatives, paper industry suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus for Timber/Construction (Multiple Species)
varieties.availability: State Forest Departments, farm forestry programs throughout tropical monsoon climates, tropical monsoon climates timber markets

varieties.name: Eucalyptus for Farm Forestry Kerala (Recommended Species)
varieties.availability: Widely available - State Forest Departments, tropical monsoon climates agricultural cooperatives, farm forestry extension services, private nurseries throughout tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Eucalyptus Species for Degraded Land Restoration
varieties.availability: Institute of Forest Genetics and Tree Breeding Coimbatore, tropical monsoon climates Forest Research Institute Peechi, advanced forestry programs
